title: cassia seleri
intro: Cassia seleri, also known as Cassia occidentalis, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Cassia seleri has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering several health benefits such as reducing pain and swelling, and treating wounds and infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and ointment, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
